## 🎯 Quick Context

**What We're Building:** ML-based network intrusion detection combining multiple algorithms  
**Current Checkpoint:** Phase - Project structure & foundation complete  
**Subject:** Major Project (6th Semester, CS with ML Specialization - Final Year)  
**Deadline:** End of semester + Defense - Late April/May 2026  
**Tech Stack:** Python 3+ with ML Libraries (TensorFlow, Scikit-Learn, Pandas)

```

├── 02_Training_Lab/            ← Model training implementations

├── 04_Testing_Evidence/        ← Testing & validation results
├── README.md                   ← Project documentation
├── requirements.txt            ← Python dependencies
└── .gitignore                  ← Git configuration
```

## 💻 TECHNICAL DETAILS - HYBRID ML APPROACH

**Why "Hybrid" IDS?**
- Uses **multiple ML algorithms** instead of single model
- Combines strengths of different approaches
- Improves detection accuracy and reduces false positives

**Likely Algorithms to Implement:**
- Random Forest (ensemble method)
- Support Vector Machine (SVM)
- Neural Networks (Deep Learning)
- Possibly Gradient Boosting (XGBoost)

**Expected Datasets:**
- NSL-KDD dataset (23 features per connection)
- UNSW-NB15 (49 features per connection)
- Contains: Normal traffic + various attack types

**Performance Metrics to Track:**
- Accuracy
- Precision & Recall (important for IDS)
- F1-Score
- ROC-AUC score
- False Positive Rate (critical for IDS)
